insight: Insights system initialized for cross-agent learning
MultiAIOrchestrator is a redirect layer to UnifiedAIOrchestrator - all imports work via backward compatibility
Quest status is normalized: 'complete' becomes 'completed' via normalize_status() in status_helpers.py
Async handlers in router need await - check with hasattr(result, '__await__') before calling .get()
System has 15 canonical terminals (10 required, 5 optional) managed by TerminalManager with state persistence in terminal_config.json
Test pass rate improved from unknown to 99.7% (1161/1164) after fixing imports and async issues
When adding test stubs to _system_handlers, must also update _valid_targets or router will default to 'auto'
prevention: Make _valid_targets dynamic or always update it when adding handlers

insight: Test tried to access harvester.sys.argv but sys wasn't imported in module - should use monkeypatch.setattr(sys, 'argv', ...) instead
VS Code 'errors' count includes pyright type checking (547 errors, 41 warnings) in addition to ruff linting - need both tools for comprehensive quality
context: User reported 148 errors but ruff showed 0 - pyright revealed 547 type errors

insight: Top pyright errors: None-checking (19 cases), missing awaits on coroutines (13 cases), type annotation issues. Pattern: need defensive None checks and proper async/await throughout
prevention: Add type guards, use Optional[] annotations, validate None before method calls

insight: get_board() was async but didn't await anything - caused coroutine return instead of GuildBoard, breaking 26 call sites
fix: Made get_board() synchronous since no async work needed

insight: dataclass fields with '| None' + __post_init__ that ensures non-None should use field(default_factory=list) instead - clearer intent, better type inference
prevention: Use field(default_factory) for mutable defaults instead of None + post_init
